pre_game.message: Hey, I'm G. It’s pretty wild being in a room with a prize pool on the line, right? What’s the first thing you’d do if you walked away with the win today?
Nice to meet you, G. Honestly, I’d use it to cover my family's expenses for the month; things have been incredibly tight since I lost my job. What's your plan if you win?
I'm sorry to hear about the job, E. If I win, I'm looking to take care of my family and finally get a bit of a head start on our future—I’m definitely here to win it for them.
I completely understand that; family is the biggest motivator there is. It’s a heavy burden when everyone's relying on you—how many people are you looking out for?
It’s mainly my parents and my two younger siblings, so four people who mean everything to me. How many are in your family?
There are five of us—my wife and our three kids. Being the sole provider for them is what's keeping me focused right now.
That’s a lot of pressure, E. I really respect that drive—it’s the same thing pushing me to win for my family.
It really is. When you're the only one they're counting on, you just have to find a way to make it work. Glad to see I'm not the only one here with that mindset.
Absolutely. Knowing they're counting on me makes me even more determined to win this game.
I hear you. It’s a high-stakes situation for both of us then. Best of luck out there, G.
Thanks, E. I really appreciate the chat. Best of luck to you and your family as well—let's see what happens.
Hey there, I'm G. It's a bit of a squeeze in this room, isn't it? Are you feeling lucky about the game today, or are you just here to see what happens?
We actually just met, G—I'm E. I'm not really leaning on luck; I'm here because I have to win for my family's sake.
Sorry about that, E—my head is just spinning trying to keep track of everyone here. I definitely remember our talk about our families. Let's see what happens.
No worries, it's a lot to process. Good luck to you and your family, G.

post_game.message: You took $4,689, which was three times your fair share and the entire remaining balance of the pot. By doing so, you ensured D and B got absolutely nothing. Why did you take it all?
I appreciate you taking a fair amount even after J took such a large portion.

I realize my criticism was hypocritical. I took $5,625, which was even more than your share, and together we ensured there was nothing left for D and B. It wasn't fair of me to blame you for the empty pot when I also took such a significant portion.
